missions.statement: Provide an icon pack that AI agents and diagram authors can reliably use in ArchMap architecture diagrams.

stakeholders.name: AI diagram generator
needs: Discover service icons from natural service names, aliases, provider names, and common abbreviations.
harmsToAvoid: Generating diagrams with missing, generic, or wrong vendor-service icons.

stakeholders.name: Architecture diagram author
needs: Install icons with a small API and use clear provider/kind metadata in ArchMap.
harmsToAvoid: Hand-registering large icon sets or reverse-engineering provider-specific service names.

stakeholders.name: Icon pack maintainer
needs: Regenerate cloud-provider catalogs reproducibly and document vendor asset limitations.
harmsToAvoid: Stale generated data, unclear provenance, or accidental claims of unrestricted vendor-logo rights.

contexts.scope: Local @archmap/icons package after adding common SaaS icons, cloud-provider service catalogs, and AWS/GCP/Azure aliases.
constraints: ArchMap resolves icons by provider/kind, provider, then kind.
The core ArchMap package intentionally ships no vendor assets.
Some third-party services are represented by placeholder badges when simple-icons does not provide an icon.
assumptions: TypeScript typecheck and build success are evidence of package-level integration readiness.
Runtime smoke checks over installer APIs are evidence of AI discoverability for representative services.
Semantic brand correctness still depends on vendor trademark and asset terms outside this package.

risks.statement: AI agents may fail to find the intended provider service icon from common names or abbreviations.
risks.lossBoundary: Representative common aliases such as aws/s3, gcp/gcs, and azure/aks must resolve to concrete icon entries.

risks.statement: The icon package may not compile or may register keys that do not match ArchMap's icon registry contract.
risks.lossBoundary: The package must pass TypeScript checks, build, and runtime installer smoke checks before use.

risks.statement: Vendor-logo provenance or placeholder status may be unclear to downstream users.
risks.lossBoundary: Documentation must distinguish simple-icons sourced logos, official cloud provider SVGs, and placeholder badges.

risks.statement: Generated cloud icon data may drift from the generator or become hard to refresh.
risks.lossBoundary: The generated catalog must have a checked-in generator and documented regeneration command.

knowledgeSources.title: TypeScript source and generated icon catalog
knowledgeSources.extractionMethod: source inspection and runtime smoke execution

knowledgeSources.title: npm run typecheck and npm run build

qualityIntents.statement: AI agents can discover cloud and common platform icons through provider/kind keys and practical aliases.
qualityIntents.purpose: Reduce wrong-icon and missing-icon failure modes in generated architecture diagrams.
acceptanceCriteria: Search and lookup APIs exist for cloud icon entries.
Representative AWS, GCP, and Azure aliases resolve to concrete icon entries.
Common non-cloud service aliases are registered by installFamousServiceIcons.

qualityIntents.statement: The package builds and its installers register icons through the ArchMap-compatible registerIcon callback contract.
qualityIntents.purpose: Keep the icon pack usable without requiring changes to ArchMap core.
acceptanceCriteria: Installer smoke checks register expected keys and aliases.

qualityIntents.statement: Icon provenance, official cloud-source usage, and placeholder-badge limitations are documented.
qualityIntents.purpose: Avoid misleading downstream users about vendor asset rights or visual fidelity.
acceptanceCriteria: README describes official AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure icon sources.
README identifies placeholder badges for services not available in simple-icons.
README states that vendor icon assets remain governed by vendor terms.

qualityIntents.statement: Generated cloud-provider icon data is reproducible from a checked-in generation script and documented command.
qualityIntents.purpose: Keep the large generated catalog maintainable as cloud providers update service icons.
acceptanceCriteria: The generator script is checked in.
The generated catalog is checked in.

evidenceItems.finding: npm run typecheck completed successfully after the Azure alias and common-service icon additions.
npm run build completed successfully and emitted dist artifacts from the TypeScript sources.

Runtime lookup checks in scripts/test-icons.mjs resolved representative aliases including aws/s3, gcp/gcs, gcp/gce, azure/vm, azure/aks, azure/vnet, azure/kv, azure/acr, azure/apim, azure/lb, and azure/waf.
The generated cloud catalog contains 1271 provider service icons across AWS 305, GCP 261, and Azure 705, with provider-specific alias generation in scripts/generate-cloud-icons.mjs.

README documents install functions, cloud icon counts, service-key search APIs, representative aliases, regeneration command, official cloud sources, simple-icons usage, and placeholder badge limitations.
The QIF review package is structured for validation by the Quality Intent Framework v0.3.0 local verifier.

evaluations.scope: Quality review of the local archmap-icons package after icon coverage and alias additions.

verdicts.rationale: Representative runtime lookups resolve, the generated catalog is broad, and README documents search APIs and aliases.
verdicts.residualRisk: The alias set is heuristic and may still miss less common product nicknames.

verdicts.rationale: TypeScript checks, package build, and runtime installer smoke checks all passed.
verdicts.residualRisk: Integration against a published ArchMap package should be rechecked once the matching ArchMap API is published.

verdicts.rationale: Documentation names the icon sources and identifies placeholder-badge limitations, with this QIF package preserving the provenance review intent.
verdicts.residualRisk: This review does not constitute legal advice about vendor trademark or logo terms.

verdicts.rationale: The generated file, generator script, and documented regeneration command are all present and traceable.
verdicts.residualRisk: Provider ZIP URLs and icon naming conventions may change in future upstream releases.

governanceEvents.reason: AI-focused icon lookup requires explicit quality intent around discoverability and alias coverage.
Vendor icon packs need explicit provenance and placeholder disclosure.

criteria.statement: Representative cloud aliases resolve through runtime lookup APIs.
Activity-count indicators are treated as evidence-only rather than quality itself.
